---
title: Rural and Nonrural Counties in the United States
description: Choropleth map showing the classification of U.S. counties as rural (nonmetro)
  or nonrural (metro).
date: '2026-05-07'
layout: default
permalink: /charts/rural-economic-outlook-map-rural-nonrural/
categories:
  - Chart
  - Geography
  - Rural Development
tags:
  - USDA-ERS
  - Rural Definition
  - Nonmetro
  - County-Level
  - Map
image: map_rural_nonrural.svg
chart:
  title: Rural and Nonrural Counties in the United States
  chartType: map
  interactive: false
  source: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Economic Research Service
  sourceUrl: https://www.ers.usda.gov/data-products/county-typology-codes/
  featured: false
producedBy: rural-economic-outlook
usesDatasets:
  - usda-county-typology
usesPackages:
  - ruraldefinitions
usesResources: []
format:
  gfm:
    toc: false
    wrap: none
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

![This reference map shows the classification of all U.S. counties as rural (nonmetro) or nonrural (metro) based on OMB definitions.](./map_rural_nonrural.svg)

## Overview

This reference map shows the classification of all U.S. counties as rural (nonmetro) or nonrural (metro) based on OMB definitions.

## Key Findings

- Approximately 72% of U.S. counties are classified as rural (nonmetro)
- Rural counties are concentrated in the Great Plains, Appalachia, and the West
- Nonrural counties cluster around major metropolitan areas

## Reproducibility

Generated by `R/viz/presentation/rural_nonrural_map.R` in the producing project.
